name: Water Curtain Cave
description: The Water Curtain Cave (Shuǐlián Dòng, 水簾洞) is a significant location in Chinese mythology, most notably featured in the classic novel 'Journey to the West'. It is a large, naturally formed cave located behind a waterfall on Mount Huaguo (Flower Fruit Mountain). The cave is the original home and kingdom of Sun Wukong, the Monkey King, and his monkey tribe. It symbolizes a place of discovery, independence, and self-determination, representing a haven where Sun Wukong established his authority and developed his skills. The cave provides shelter and resources, allowing the monkeys to thrive. Its location behind the waterfall signifies a hidden world, separate from the mundane and accessible only to those who dare to venture beyond the ordinary. The Water Curtain Cave is not merely a geographical location but a potent symbol of ambition, leadership, and the pursuit of personal growth. It is a foundational element in Sun Wukong's origin story, marking the beginning of his legendary journey. The discovery and subsequent claim of the cave as his territory solidify his status as a leader and lay the groundwork for his future adventures. It represents a space of autonomy and self-governance, free from the constraints of the celestial bureaucracy that Sun Wukong would later challenge.
subtitle: Monkey King's original home and kingdom

keyMyths.title: Discovery of the Cave
keyMyths.description: Sun Wukong and his monkey tribe discover the Water Curtain Cave after a daring leap through the waterfall. He declares that whoever can jump through the waterfall and return safely will be their king.

keyMyths.title: Establishment of the Kingdom
keyMyths.description: After successfully jumping through the waterfall, Sun Wukong becomes the king of the monkeys and establishes his kingdom within the Water Curtain Cave. He leads his tribe and enjoys a life of freedom and abundance.

sources.author: Wu Cheng'en
sources.description: Classic Chinese novel featuring Sun Wukong and the Water Curtain Cave.
sources.text: Classic Chinese novel featuring Sun Wukong and the Water Curtain Cave.
